review:
Total War: Warhammer III is what happens when a strategy series fully embraces the fact that subtlety is overrated and that maybe the correct amount of fantasy warfare is “more.”

More monsters. More artillery. More insanity. More factions. More spectacle. More completely unreasonable battlefield decisions that somehow become legendary victories.

And somehow, instead of collapsing under its own excess, it works.

What makes Warhammer 3 special is the sheer confidence of the setting and faction design. Every faction feels like it’s playing a slightly different game. Not fake asymmetry where one faction gets +10% archers and another gets +10% taxes. Actual identity. Kislev feels stubborn and doomed in the best way. Cathay feels orderly and monumental. Chaos feels like the laws of reality filing for bankruptcy.

And then there are the Chaos Dwarfs.

Absolute industrialized evil. Magnificent.

The Chaos Dwarfs are maybe the single best faction Creative Assembly has ever made because every mechanic reinforces exactly who they are. Ruthless, hyper-industrial, cruel beyond reason, and completely unapologetic about it. Your economy runs on slavery, smoke, steel, and the kind of logistical brutality that would make a Victorian coal baron uncomfortable.

And it’s glorious.

The entire campaign feels like managing an evil war machine that constantly demands more labor, more weapons, more fuel, more expansion. Convoys, factories, infernal industry, towering daemon-forges, artillery that looks like it was designed by somebody asking “what if a train hated humanity?” It all fits together perfectly.

The battlefield experience is even better. Few things in strategy gaming compare to watching disciplined lines of infernal guard hold the center while magma cannons and dreadquake mortars turn the horizon into an industrial accident. Then bull centaurs crash into a flank while hobgoblins commit whatever crimes hobgoblins commit when unsupervised.

The scale of Warhammer 3 also deserves credit. Immortal Empires feels less like a campaign map and more like a giant fantasy sandbox where every campaign eventually spirals into mythology. Tiny regional conflicts become continent-wide wars against gods, undead monarchs, rat civilizations, or some screaming demon prince with six axes and emotional issues.

But underneath all the spectacle, the strategy layer is still strong. Diplomacy matters. Economy matters. Army composition matters. Positioning matters. The game succeeds because there’s an actual tactical foundation supporting the absurdity.

Most importantly, Warhammer 3 understands that fantasy strategy games should occasionally feel overwhelming in the best possible way. Not balanced into sterile symmetry. Not reduced to esports minimalism. Grand, ridiculous, thematic warfare where your screen becomes complete chaos and you somehow still pull victory out of the fire.

Hashut approves. And honestly, that’s enough for me.

review:
Bonjour à toutes et à tous, je ne fais jamais de commentaire sur quoi que ce soit, mais quand je vois certains com' de plusieurs joueurs, je me sentais obligé de réagir. 
Je réagis surtout suite aux com' pour le 8.0.
Vous avez oublié le chemin parcouru. Comme certains d'entre vous, j'ai pu m'éclater sur les total war historiques, tels que Napoléon, Empire, Rome 2, Attila, le triste Pharoah et le ultra punitif Shogun 2 xD.
Dès l'annonce du premier TWW, j'étais fou. 
Dès l'arrivée de TWW 2, j'étais aux anges. 
Et comme beaucoup, j'ai été déçu à l'arrivée de TWW 3.
Creative Assembly a merdé sur pas mal de points: sa communication, son suivi réel des ressentis des joueurs et ainsi de suite. 
Je ne reviendrais pas sur les prix des DLC, les contenus. Il y a eu bien assez de romans écrits sur ce sujet.
Oui il y a des bugs, des problèmes d'équilibrages, de délai sur les correctifs apportés et leurs quantités. 
Mais mince quoi... On a la possibilité de jouer à l'une des adaptation sur PC de l'un des univers de Fantasy les plus garnis de cette planète.
Au vu de la quantité de lore, d'unité qui prennent vie via notre souris, êtes-vous réellement surpris qu'il y ai des problèmes d'équilibrage ?
Les bugs font parti de la vie des jeux. Plus il est volumineux et dense en termes de données, d'unités, forcément ça suit.
Creative Assembly travaille sur plusieurs projets : TW Medieval 3, TW W40k, TWW3 lords of the ends times, les DLC et les correctifs.
Sans oublier la pression de Sega suite à certains échecs comme Hyena.
Lorsque vous laissez un commentaire haineux, ne vous posez-vous jamais la question de l'impact ?
Oui nous attendons toutes et tous un retour sur investissement lorsque l'on achète un jeu et ses DLC.
Mais de par mon vécu professionnel, je ne peux m'empêcher de me poser certaines questions : 
quel effectif sur quel projet ? quel réel support pour l'équipe travaillant sur les correctifs par exemple ?
Vous ne savez rien de leur quotidien, la pression exercée aussi bien en interne qu'en externe.
Et vous crachez à la figure de ceux qui, tant bien que mal, ont mis dans nos mains un jeux-vidéo Fantasy sans précédent. Bravo.
Vous avez oublié les débuts de TWW 1 ? 5 factions jouables, deux seigneurs légendaires par faction et on s'éclatait déjà bien ! 
On en est à cb aujourd'hui ? Regardez en arrière. Oui il y a des fautes, mais ne venez pas chier sur leur gueule pour autant.
CA a merdé, s'adapte et fait au mieux, je n'en doute pas. Perso, je fais le choix de garder espoir.
à bon entendeur, salut.

review_english: Hello everyone, I never comment on anything, but when I see certain comments from several players, I felt obliged to react. I react mainly following the comments for 8.0. You have forgotten how far you have come. Like some of you, I had a blast playing historical total war games, such as Napoleon, Empire, Rome 2, Attila, the sad Pharoah and the ultra punitive Shogun 2 xD. As soon as the first TWW was announced, I was crazy. As soon as TWW 2 arrived, I was in heaven. And like many, I was disappointed when TWW 3 arrived. Creative Assembly screwed up on a lot of points in its communication, its real monitoring of players' feelings and so on. I won't go into the prices of the DLC or the content. There have been enough novels written on this subject. Yes there are bugs, balancing problems, delays in the fixes made and their quantities. But what the hell... We have the opportunity to play one of the PC adaptations of one of the most extensive Fantasy universes on this planet. Given the amount of lore and unity that comes to life via our mouse, are you really surprised that there are balancing issues? Bugs are part of the life of games. The more voluminous and dense it is in terms of data, units, obviously it follows. Creative Assembly is working on several projects TW Medieval 3, TW W40k, TWW3 lords of the ends times, DLC and patches. Without forgetting the pressure from Sega following certain failures like Hyena. When you leave a hateful comment, do you ever ask yourself the question of the impact? Yes, we all expect a return on investment when we buy a game and its DLC. But from my professional experience, I can't help but ask myself certain questions: what staff on what project? what real support for the team working on the fixes for example? You know nothing about their daily life, the pressure exerted both internally and externally. And you spit in the face of those who, as best they could, put an unprecedented Fantasy video game in our hands. Well done. Have you forgotten the beginnings of TWW 1? 5 playable factions, two legendary lords per faction and we were already having a blast! Are we at CB today? Look back. Yes there are mistakes, but don't come and shit on them. CA screwed up, adapts and does the best it can, I have no doubt. Personally, I choose to keep hope. word to the wise, hello.

review:
缺点是我感觉比前两作的兼容性差一些，遇到过好几次闪退了，无论是A卡还是N卡。
但是游戏性还是很不错的，比前两作势力更多，内容也更丰富一点。

中国玩家买锤三主要就是因为有震旦。这里必须表扬一下，震旦势力没有用满清野猪人和蒙古蛋子的元素，美工水平也棒，人物在锤系审美里算是很美观了，势力背景设定也不拉胯，很容易联想到宋军和明军，冲这个美工和文化上的尊重，必须支持一波。另外gw的战棋，tow和40k也是花了接近一万了，咱这里尊重中国汉族玩家的结果就是闷声发大财。

写在这期DLC：希望后边可以避免furry元素，坚持以东亚汉族人的角色为本，战锤玩家不是二次元。增加职业，性格和背景人设的多样化远比搞一堆熊猫人老虎人什么的有意义。比如日本的忍者和武士元素是很丰富很多人喜欢的，为什么不能引入一些中国周代和汉代武士背景的角色呢？万军从中一换一的狠角色，人气不会差的。之前的陶俑巨兽是很棒的美术设计，这样类似的有文物和文化做背景设计出来的角色，本身就更真实和更有代入感。暴雪魔兽世界的熊猫人DLC是他很糟糕的一步棋，就是因为美术元素的割裂和低代入感导致的吗，希望gw在这方面不要重蹈覆辙。另外老生常谈的中国神话角色建议避开，因为你是战锤宇宙，如果完全采用等同于中国神话宇宙的角色，就会产生严重的割裂感和审美疲劳。我已经在十几个外国开发团队的游戏里见到孙悟空和哪吒这些人了，我不希望他们又出现在全战震旦势力里（虽然故事里好像孙悟空已经有了？）。偏真实魔化风格的游戏，要避免过于神话的故事设定和审美疲劳。希望gw这方面多做思考。

review_english: The disadvantage is that I feel that the compatibility is worse than the previous two games. I have encountered several crashes. Regardless of whether it is an A card or an N card, the gameplay is still very good. It has more factions than the previous two games, and the content is richer. Chinese players mainly buy Hammer III because of Cathay. I must praise it. The Cathay faction does not use the elements of Manchu wild boars and Mongolian eggs. The art level is also great. The characters are very beautiful in the Hammer series aesthetic. The background settings of the forces are not too crotch-like. It is easy to think of the Song Army and the Ming Army. For this artistic and cultural respect, we must support it. In addition, GW's war chess ToW and 40k also cost close to 10,000. The result of respecting Chinese Han players here is to make a fortune silently. I wrote this DLC in the hope that the furry element can be avoided in the future. We insist on focusing on the roles of East Asian Han people. Warhammer players are not two-dimensional. Adding professions, diversifying characters and backgrounds is far more meaningful than making a bunch of Pandaren and Tigermen. For example, Japanese ninja and samurai elements are very rich and many people like them. Why can't we introduce some characters with Chinese warrior backgrounds from the Zhou Dynasty and Han Dynasty? Wan Jun is a ruthless character who has been replaced by one. The popularity will not be bad. The previous ceramic behemoths are great art designs. Such similar characters with cultural relics and cultural backgrounds are more realistic and immersive. Blizzard's Pandaren DLC in World of Warcraft is a very bad move. Is it because of the fragmentation of art elements and low sense of immersion? I hope gw will not repeat the same mistakes in this regard. In addition, it is recommended to avoid clichéd Chinese mythological characters because you are in the Warhammer universe. If you completely use characters equivalent to the Chinese mythological universe, it will cause a serious sense of fragmentation and aesthetic fatigue. I have seen people like Sun Wukong and Nezha in more than a dozen foreign development teams’ games. I don’t want them to appear in Total War Cathay again. Although the story seems to have Sun Wukong already, games with a realistic demonization style should avoid overly mythical story settings and aesthetic fatigue. I hope gw will think more about this aspect

review:
At it's current state it's really boring and unbalanced.
The AI attacks only unwalled settlements which makes it a cat and mouse chase game, since CA in it's infinite wisdom removed minor settlements walls.
The AI also goes out of it's way to go around your armies, and not attacking them.(Total Chase: Warhammer 3)
The AI's AntiPlayer Bias is outright silly, and makes you run around putting out fires which removes the grand strategy layer or at least reduce it significally.
The Vassal system and Alliance system is broken. 
As soon as you ally or subjucate a faction, they become totally braindead.
The AI cheats is also out of whack!

In it's state i will not recommend the game, it's broken and unbalanced.
I really used to love this series, but it needs alot of fixes AI and campaign wise!

review:
I bought the "Total War: Warhammer III Collection" bundle idiotically believing that it would unlock all of the content for the game. What a fool I was. CREATIVE ASSEMBLY and Games Workshop are greedy. How can you hope for a fair, clear sale from the greedy? What a grand and intoxicating innocence. How could I be so naive? There is no deal.

Buying the $85.22 bundle--on sale 55% off, by the way, $190.68 normally, at the time of writing (it WILL go higher as they churn out more DLC)--does not actually include all of the DLC for this game. But even if you did then fill in all of the gaps by buying the rest of this game's DLC, you would still be missing content, because guess what? Want to play even the most basic, straightforward factions in Warhammer, like, I don't know, the Empire? Well sorry, your only option for the Empire is some totally-not-a-vampire-chick--the actual Emperor Karl Franz is off limits, along with most other factions in their ENTIRETY--unless you also buy BOTH of their previous Total War: Warhammer games, AND the DLC for those games in order to ACTUALY actually unlock everything. I think, anyway. Maybe their is some secret extra DLC I'll find out the game is withholding from me later on, they've done it once before already, after all.

The way they're chopping up their game so they can sell the parts of it that were already in the previous game back to you piecemeal nearly rivals EA in it's brazen greed--the only thing that keeps it from being on par being the fact that if you already owned the the previous games and their DLC, then I guess you don't need to rebuy it.

Also, just to be crystal clear, abhorrent DLC practices aside, even if I had gotten the game and all of its DLC for free (and all of the previous games and their DLC too, since again you need that for all this game's content), I still wouldn't recommend the game on the basis of quality either. At its best, it's just fine. But generally it's worse than Total War: SHOGUN 2, a game in same Total War series that came out over 2 decades earlier. The series doesn't just seem to have not made any progress in ten+ years, but has actually regressed significantly. This game has worse balance, worse gameplay, is bloated with ability micromanagement, uninteresting tech and skill trees, generally shallower settlement management, and dozens of missing features that have been inexplicably been removed, like the ability to manage the shape/closeness of units to adapt to archer fire or melee combat, cutscenes for hero actions, pre-battle speeches. They made recruitment worse, because units can't navigate the map without being in a lord's army, so when you call for recruitment from an army, instead of the unit being created and then walking on it's own to your army, the army is frozen for several turns while your reinforcements are bottlenecked through a faction wide shared, magic teleporting "global recruitment". Or you do what I do and have a scuffed system of "recruitment jockey" lords who's only job is ferrying back and forth units to your front line. Moving multiple units uniformly and precisely as a formation used to have a convenient button panel, but now it's hidden away behind hotkeys that you've got to look up every ten seconds. It also has gamebreaking bugs, like for example, when entering the realm of Chaos, the game might just softlock by having a cutscene never end, leaving you with letterboxing covering 50% of your screen, and no way to open the game's menu.

review:
Este juego es una maravilla, es innegable, tiene unas dimensiones absurdamente inabarcables, eso explica que lleve casi 180 horas jugadas, y aún me faltarán 180 horas más por jugar. 

Me he ceñido a jugar las campañas con las diversas facciones, ya que cada una integra elementos propios, que alteran ligeramente la campaña, algunas misiones propias, finales alternativos, etc. He de decir que en ese sentido hace que el juego sea rejugable casi hasta el infinito. Además está la parte multijugador para quien le interese, a mi personalmente solo me interesa jugar campaña para relajarme y pasar el rato. 

El juego presenta una sección de gestión, en la que comprar edificios, tropas, y mover los ejercitos de un lado a otro. Y otra sección de batallas, en las cuales se pone especialmente táctico, y hay que buscar un posicionamiento idóneo para tu estrategia. Como en este tipo de juegos puedes centrarte en la primera fase y saltar las batallas de forma automática, o jugarlas (aunque esto alarga ya la alargada campaña)

En cuanto a la ambientación en el mundo warhammer es perfecta, e incluso la amplia introduciendo a Cathay como ejercito jugable, con una interpretación propia y singular. 

Por lo que es un juego recomendable.

Puntos positivos.
-Warhammer, fidedigno, tanto en la ambientación como en el propio juego de mesa, se asemeja mucho, en cuanto al posicionamiento de las unidades, la configuración de ejércitos, los objetos etc, aunque es en tiempo real y no por turnos.
-Rejugabilidad absurdamente amplia.
-Varias facciones con sus propias estrategias, algunas muy locas, otras más lógicas, en esencia, hay ejércitos de un sólo héroe (Vlad, Teclis, Skarbrand), ejércitos de artillería, (Imperio, Enanos, Cathay), ejércitos de confrontación frontal y masa, (Caos, demonios del Caos, Orkos, Bretonia), ejércitos de magia (Altos Elfos, Tzeentch), ejércitos de hostigamiento (altos elfos, elfos oscuros). Y aún con esos arquetipos se pueden configurar ejércitos diferentes dentro de cada facción, sobre todo en función a cada comandante. 
-Historia, pues no tiene como tal, si bien el mapa está lleno de detalles, y cada comandante tiene sus propias misiones que narran muy ligeramente la historia de los mismos. Si ya estás en el mundo te enteras de que van, si no tienes idea de Warhammer se va a quedar muy triste.
-Da mucha satisfacción usar a Vlad o a Teclis contra los Skavens, y que ellos solos eliminen a 2000 o 3000 ratas, por no hablar de que es la mejor forma de conseguir algún logro de steam. 

Puntos malos.
-El precio, es ridiculo la cantidad de DLC que tiene, es cierto que en mi caso por ejemplo, al ponerlo en la lista de deseados, Steam a la semana me hizo una rebaja del 87%, por lo que por unos 100 euros pude adquirir todos los DLC, pero sin oferta, ni pack, el precio es absurdo, y la versión base del juego se queda corta, muy poca variedad de ejercitos.
-La diplomacia está trucada para que en la partida los buenos vayan con los buenos, los malos con los malos, es cierto que tiene su lógica en la historia pero le quita gracia al asunto. Intenté seriamente hacer una partida de Vlad aliado al Imperio, y fue imposible, creo que hay opciones para modificar algunas cosas de la partida, pero yo personalmente no las he encontrado. 
-Hay campañas que se hacen muy simples de ganar, y otras muy difíciles, casi se puede ver como un modo de dificultad añadido, pero considero seriamente que es casi imposible vencer algunas partidas, si un rival concreto no ha sido eliminado en las fases iniciales del juego. 

En fin, un juegazo para los amantes de warhammer, que queda como lo más parecido al juego de mesa que fue.

review_english: This game is a marvel, it is undeniable, it has absurdly unfathomable dimensions, which explains why I have played it for almost 180 hours, and I still have 180 more hours to play. I have stuck to playing the campaigns with the various factions, since each one integrates its own elements, which slightly alter the campaign, some own missions, alternative endings, etc. I have to say that in that sense it makes the game replayable almost infinitely. There is also the multiplayer part for those who are interested, personally I am only interested in playing the campaign to relax and pass the time. The game features a management section, in which you buy buildings, troops, and move armies from one place to another. And another section of battles, in which it becomes especially tactical, and you have to find an ideal positioning for your strategy. As in this type of game, you can focus on the first phase and skip the battles automatically, or play them (although this already lengthens the long campaign). As for the setting in the Warhammer world, it is perfect, and even the extensive one introducing Cathay as a playable army, with its own unique interpretation. So it is a recommended game. Positive points. -Warhammer, reliable, both in the setting and in the board game itself, is very similar, in terms of the positioning of the units, the configuration of armies, the objects, etc., although it is in real time and not in turns. -Absurdly extensive replayability. -Various factions with their own strategies, some very crazy, others more logical, in essence, there are armies of a single hero (Vlad, Teclis, Skarbrand), artillery armies (Empire, Dwarves, Cathay), armies of frontal and mass confrontation, (Chaos, Chaos demons, Orks, Bretonnia), armies of magic (High Elves, Tzeentch), harassment armies (high elves, elves dark). And even with these archetypes, different armies can be configured within each faction, especially depending on each commander. -History, as it does not have as such, although the map is full of details, and each commander has their own missions that very lightly narrate their history. If you are already in the world you find out what they are about, if you have no idea about Warhammer you will be very sad. -It is very satisfying to use Vlad or Teclis against the Skaven, and for them alone to eliminate 2000 or 3000 rats, not to mention that it is the best way to get some steam achievement. Bad points. -The price, the amount of DLC it has is ridiculous, it is true that in my case for example, when I put it on the wish list, Steam gave me a discount of 87 per week, so for about 100 euros I could purchase all the DLC, but without an offer, or pack, the price is absurd, and the base version of the game falls short, very little variety of armies. -Diplomacy is tricked so that in the game the good guys go with the good guys, the bad guys with the bad guys, it is true that it has its logic in the story but it takes away the fun of the matter. I seriously tried to make a game of Vlad allied to the Empire, and it was impossible, I think there are options to modify some things in the game, but I personally have not found them. -There are campaigns that are very easy to win, and others very difficult, it can almost be seen as an added difficulty mode, but I seriously consider that it is almost impossible to win some games, if a specific rival has not been eliminated in the initial phases of the game. In short, a great game for Warhammer lovers, which is the closest thing to the board game it was.

review:
Years and years of work and the game feels stagnated. I just don't like where the game is at. A lot of focus has been given to huge issues like monetization and DLC structure. But even beyond that I just don't care for any of the campaign maps or how the world is presented. 

The Old World covered in chaos absolutely everywhere just isn't as interesting to me, there's no interesting conflicts between the Empire and Brettonia since both are busy fending off chaos attacks. It's all just so... boring.

I've gotten a lot of hours out of this game. But it seems doomed to be the worst of the three and the most dissapointing by far, no matter what the devs promise.

review:
My first ever Total War game and I’m genuinely loving it.  
Warhammer 3 is huge, chaotic, and ridiculously fun once you get into it. The battles feel epic, the factions all play differently, and the amount of content is honestly insane for a newcomer.

Playing with friends is where the game really shines. Co‑op campaigns are addictive, every turn feels like something big is happening, and the mix of strategy + real‑time battles keeps things exciting.

Performance could be a bit better the optimisation isn’t perfect but it still runs well enough to enjoy, and the gameplay more than makes up for it.

If you’re new to Total War like me, don’t be scared off. It’s a blast to learn, even better with friends, and absolutely worth jumping into.

Как фанату Warhammer, если закрыть глаза на то что у игры есть косяки — Ничего лучше и эпичнее вы не найдете, ну разве-что какие-нибудь моды на Mount and Blade, и то это моды и они не идут в качество с лицензионным продуктом.
Меня не устраивает одно — Вылеты. 
У меня топовое железо для этой игры, но она умудряется вылетать когда я завершаю ход, а все потому что хвалёный движок Бриташек за какую-то из стран пытается что-то пернуть и высрать, но у него не получается и надо обязательно крашнуть игру.
А решений как таковых нет, ты либо откатываешься на сейвы раньше, либо достаёшь читы и ломаешь ♥♥♥♥♥ компу, тогда и о чудо вылеты исчезают.
Я понимаю если это была-бы инди игра, от 1.5 разработчика, но это целая студия с штатом сотрудников, что делает ААА игры, они монополисты, потому-что ничего прям вот такого нет, и вместо того чтобы собрать новый и современный движок, они тянут старый 2007 года, которому уже 19 лет, он не тянет ряд вещей и наворотов, тот-же DLSS/FSR, Генерацию кадров, хотя такой игре с обилием модов и кучки юнитов на экране это нужно.
Почему с релизом нельзя было починить игру или сохранения, мне непонятно.
А ещё вспомнил — Каждый раз когда я сажусь играть катку, обязательно разрабы должны выкатить патч и длс, которые ломают мегамоды по типу Radius или SFO. Вы наверное скажите - "так откатись и доигрывай катку, в чем проблема" А проблема в разрабах других модов, что берут и обновляют под новый патч и откат не дает доиграть, потому-что мод уже под новую версию построен.

review_english: As a Warhammer fan, if you close your eyes to the fact that the game has flaws, you won’t find anything better or more epic, well, perhaps some mods for Mount and Blade, and then these are mods and they do not match the quality of a licensed product. One thing I'm not happy with is the departures. I have top-end hardware for this game, but it manages to crash when I complete a move, and all because the vaunted British engine for some country is trying to fart something and shit, but it doesn’t work and it’s necessary to crash the game. But there are no solutions as such, you either roll back to saves earlier, or get cheats and break your computer, then, miraculously, the crashes disappear. I understand if it were an indie game, from 1.5 developers, but this is a whole studio with a staff that makes AAA games, they are a monopoly, because there is nothing like that, and instead of putting together a new and modern engine, they use the old one from 2007, which is already 19 years old, it does not support a number of things and bells and whistles, the same DLSS FSR, Frame generation, although such a game with an abundance of mods and a bunch of units on the screen need it. Why it was impossible to fix the game or saves with the release is not clear to me. I also remembered. Every time I sit down to play the game, the developers must roll out a patch and DLC that breaks megamods like Radius or SFO. You’ll probably say, “So roll back and finish playing the game, what’s the problem?” But the problem is with the developers of other mods, who take it and update it for a new patch and roll back doesn’t allow you to finish the game, because the mod has already been built for the new version.

review:
The game is really amazing but i really dislike the monetisation of the game.
-unlike other games like stellaris. The dlc cant be shared over multiplayer campaigns. Its essentially double to triple dipping into savings. Its  a practise i really dislike and have avoided encouraging.
If you come late to the series , the game is a big investment for those who wanna try all the races.

-  gameplay wise , the game is very fun and enjoyable. with alot of factions to choose from (most you have to buy from previous games). Campaigns can take 100-300 turns so you are in for the long haul with faction gameplay. each faction has an interesting mechanic for example : cathay (ying and yang ), chaos dwarves (money, resources, minerals and seats of power that give buffs), empire (confederating the empire together to fight against chaos with alot of politicing.)

-units are really varied and fun. this usually synchs with a faction theme as well.

review:
Bhashiva was nerfed less than 24 hours after release.

My issue is not simply the balance change itself, but the way CA responds to community pressure.

The moment parts of the Western player base complained, CA reacted almost instantly. Meanwhile, feedback and preferences from Asian players often seem far less acknowledged.

This makes me question whether CA applies the same standards to all regions of its community, or whether certain cultural perspectives are being prioritized over others.

Players paid for a finished DLC release, not a temporary test build subject to immediate reactionary nerfs.

If CA continues responding selectively to only the loudest parts of the community, I will reconsider supporting future DLC content.

Yesterday, CA came back from a business trip with bad news: after being sent away to fix bugs for three years and still failing to finish the job, he got fired. Little CA was devastated. To cheer him up, his parents took him to watch a movie.

On the way home, they passed through a dark alley. Six legs went in, but only two legs came out.

And that was the origin story of Bitchman-CA.
昨天CA出差回来了，带来了一个坏消息：因为外派去修BUG修了3年没修完，他被解雇了，小CA很伤心。父母为了安慰他带他去看了电影，回来的路上经过了一个小巷子，6条腿进去2条腿出来，这就是Bitchman-CA的起源故事。CA你TM全家都死了，不到24h就硬削弱是吧

review:
I have....a lot....of hours in this game and WH2 - my account does not reflect that if you look though. I also did not 'receive the game for free' - maybe it says that because of the family library now. 

I think everyone thought or hoped this game was going to get the WH2 treatment - that is that CA would take a few years and polish this into a gem. 

Well, that still hasn't happened. 

For most of this game's lifecycle it (and Creative Assembly) were treated as a cash-cow so owner SEGA could make up for horrible decision making and business practices elsewhere. CA also had horrible communication with the player-base and ultimately put out some bad/overpriced DLC so they're not blameless. 

Shadows of Change was the first TWWH DLC I didn't rush to immediately buy in years - so many poorly thought out design choices and overpriced of course. I stopped buying the DLCs and stopped playing entirely around Omens of Destruction. This game became totally stale, and the numerous mods required to smooth out the gameplay experience became out of date eventually. 

None of the major issues with the game are resolved today.
- The AI is still completely braindead tactically in most battle situations which do not involve defending choke points. 
- Sieges overall are either boring slog through poorly designed tower defense, or way too easy with above mentioned braindead AI. The map designs are very basic, and horrific for gameplay. 
- As of the last time I played Artillery still cannot go on the walls of cities, and while CA claimed there was some redesign to allow for dedicated spots in city maps, it was poorly thought out and rushed so it didn't really work. There are several factions (dwarves as an example) which heavily depend on use of artillery so this does matter. 
- Diplomacy and AI behavior on the map were/are still totally basic and broken. I think it took CA around 3 years to fix the issue of defending your vassals earning you disrepute in diplomacy. A modder had that fixed like day-1. 
- The biggest problem. AI factions are given insane cheats to compensate for how poor the AI is designed/performs in all areas. The AI will spawn and maintain doomstacks at you even when every other city they had was razed to the ground. That completely erases any feeling of strategic gameplay on the map. 

How many $15+ USD DLCs came out in the last two years while the above was never fixed? CA told the fanbase at one point they realized they screwed up - but since that sad apology they have really just dragged their feet and tried placating people by releasing their overpriced DLC as parts you can selectively purchase. 

Let's all hope Med3 isn't given the same treatment. I certainly won't be rushing to buy or pre-order it until reviews are out.

The map designs are very basic, and horrific for gameplay. - As of the last time I played Artillery still cannot go on the walls of cities, and while CA claimed there was some redesign to allow for dedicated spots in city maps, it was poorly thought out and rushed so it didn't really work. There are several factions (dwarves as an example) which heavily depend on use of artillery so this does matter. - Diplomacy and AI behavior on the map were are still totally basic and broken. I think it took CA around 3 years to fix the issue of defending your vassals earning you disrepute in diplomacy. A modder had that fixed like day-1. - The biggest problem. AI factions are given insane cheats to compensate for how poor the AI is designed performs in all areas. The AI will spawn and maintain doomstacks at you even when every other city they had was razed to the ground. That completely erases any feeling of strategic gameplay on the map. How many 15 USD DLCs came out in the last two years while the above was never fixed? CA told the fanbase at one point they realized they screwed up - but since that sad apology they have really just dragged their feet and tried placating people by releasing their overpriced DLC as parts you can selectively purchase. Let's all hope Med3 isn't given the same treatment. I certainly won't be rushing to buy or pre-order it until reviews are out.

For most of this game's lifecycle it (and Creative Assembly) were treated as a cash-cow so owner SEGA could make up for horrible decision making and business practices elsewhere. CA also had horrible communication with the player-base and ultimately put out some bad overpriced DLC so they're not blameless. Shadows of Change was the first TWWH DLC I didn't rush to immediately buy in years - so many poorly thought out design choices and overpriced of course. review: Just like the older Warhammer games, this one is an absolute blast. The graphics look fantastic and the actual gameplay is solid. There is something so satisfying about building up your empire from scratch and juggling your economy, public order, and armies. Plus, nothing beats the feeling of completely wiping out some random enemy that had the nerve to declare war on you out of nowhere. The battles can take a minute to get used to, but they are incredibly fun once it clicks. Overall, it’s a great game and I’d highly recommend grabbing it if you’re into strategy stuff.

review:
I've played 500+ hours of WH2 and 175+ hours of WH3. The campaign has become too congested, too big, and with too many mechanics, too many new factions (and more to come). WH1 factions (BRETONNIA) still haven't been brough up to WH3 standards. It feels like quantity over quality.

Sieges just suck in this game whether attacking or defending. I actively auto-resovle even if it means losing units because it is that much of a grind.

The QOL and UI is better than WH2 but that's about all that is an improvement.

The game is still fun for the most part, but feels like a step backwards since WH2 overall.

review:
Esse jogo pode parecer meio estranho e confuso de entender como ele realmente funciona, mas por trás dessa estranheza mora um bolo de chocolate delicioso e que dá água na boca, algo que estive procurando, mas que nem eu mesmo sabia o que queria.
O estilo dele é parecido com o jogo Civilization. Você escolhe uma facção, escolhe dentre essas facções um senhor lendário, que é tipo o chefão da sua facção, e é jogado num mapa tipo um tabuleiro com outras 200 facções diferentes num mapa colossal.
O que me pegou é a variedade de raças e o estilo de cada uma. Cada facção, e às vezes até o senhor que você escolhe, muda o estilo de gameplay, e cada um tem características únicas que as diferem das demais.
E a cereja do bolo que me fez gostar mais dele que Civilization foi o combate, que não é simples movimento no tabuleiro. Aqui em Warhammer você bota suas tropas pro combate mesmo e enfrenta os inimigos montando táticas e estratégias para derrotar o exército do inimigo.
E cada unidade do seu exército tem suas particularidades e vantagens sobre outras, e isso que é maravilhoso. Tipo, você tá jogando com a facção dos vampiros, com um exército de mortos-vivos, esqueletos, morcegos gigantes, cavalarias fantasma e heróis vampiros montados em cavalos-zumbi voadores que soltam feitiços de larga escala em homens-lagarto com um exército de dinossauros e homens-jacaré com alabardas e pterodáctilos.
Onde você teria uma experiência de controlar um exército tão caótico e fantástico que nem nesse jogo?
Cara, cada facção é muito única e com um estilo de gameplay. Tem ratos sorrateiros que criam cidades escondidas subterrâneas em território inimigo, destruindo a facção por dentro e atacando em emboscadas; anões que vivem nas montanhas e que têm rixas entre outros clãs de anões para resolver; vampiros que reanimam mortos no combate e, quanto mais abates tiverem, mais fortes eles ficam; senhores do caos que enganam, seduzem ou simplesmente aniquilam outras facções para agradar seus deuses; elfos nobres que criam intrigas diplomáticas entre facções para deteriorar relações estrangeiras e têm dragões em seus exércitos; elfos negros que utilizam escravos para fazer rituais; e mais uma tonelada de outras facções e senhores com detalhes únicos.
Claro que nem tudo são flores. Na minha opinião, o principal problema dele é o sistema de monetização, que é muito repulsivo e deseja cada mísero centavo que você ganha como repositor do Atacadão.
O Warhammer 3 te disponibiliza algumas facções e alguns senhores, mas para jogar com as outras você precisa ter os outros 2 jogos anteriores e mais algumas DLCs para liberar outras, como os Homens-Besta.
Mas não se engane: quando você compra os jogos e as DLCs, você não recebe todos os senhores. O jogo tem um milhão de DLCs só com senhores. Cada senhor muda a gameplay? Sim. Mas mesmo assim você precisa pagar por uma boa parte deles.
Isso acaba afastando boa parte dos players, especialmente os brasileiros.
Mas, sendo bem honesto, somente em ter o jogo base você já tem muitas facções e senhores para testar. Caso queira jogar com mais facções, recomendo que pegue apenas os outros 2 jogos. Com eles você já vai ter uns 70% do que o jogo pode oferecer.
Dou nota 7,8 para o jogo. Ele é maravilhoso e, se não tomar cuidado, você vai trancar seu curso, faculdade e largar o emprego somente para viver no mundo desse jogo. Vai passando os turnos e você nem percebe.
Porém, esse sistema de monetização é bem repulsivo na minha opinião. Se não fosse por isso, eu daria um 8,8.
Espero que fique mais famoso aqui no Brasil e que sua comunidade brasileira cresça muito.

Ele é maravilhoso e, se não tomar cuidado, você vai trancar seu curso, faculdade e largar o emprego somente para viver no mundo desse jogo. Vai passando os turnos e você nem percebe. Porém, esse sistema de monetização é bem repulsivo na minha opinião. Se não fosse por isso, eu daria um 8,8. Espero que fique mais famoso aqui no Brasil e que sua comunidade brasileira cresça muito.
review_english: This game may seem a little strange and confusing to understand how it really works, but behind this strangeness lives a delicious and mouth-watering chocolate cake, something I've been looking for, but even I didn't know what I wanted. Its style is similar to the game Civilization. You choose a faction, choose a legendary lord from among these factions, who is like the boss of your faction, and you are played on a map like a board with 200 other different factions on a colossal map. What got me is the variety of breeds and the style of each one. Each faction, and sometimes even the lord you choose, changes the gameplay style, and each one has unique characteristics that set them apart from the others. And the icing on the cake that made me like it more than Civilization was the combat, which isn't just movement on the board. Here in Warhammer you send your troops into combat and face enemies by putting together tactics and strategies to defeat the enemy's army. And each unit in your army has its own particularities and advantages over others, and that's what's wonderful. Like, you're playing as the vampire faction, with an army of undead, skeletons, giant bats, ghost cavalry and vampire heroes riding flying zombie horses that unleash large-scale spells on lizardmen with an army of dinosaurs and alligator men with halberds and pterodactyls. Where would you have the experience of controlling an army as chaotic and fantastic as in this game? Man, each faction is very unique and has a gameplay style. There are sneaky rats who create hidden cities underground in enemy territory, destroying the faction from within and attacking in ambushes dwarves who live in the mountains and who have feuds between other clans of dwarves to resolve vampires who reanimate dead in combat and, the more kills they have, the stronger they become lords of chaos who deceive, seduce or simply annihilate other factions to please their gods noble elves who create diplomatic intrigues between factions to deteriorate relations foreign and have dragons in their armies, dark elves who use slaves to perform rituals and a ton of other factions and lords with unique details. Of course, not everything is rosy. In my opinion, its main problem is the monetization system, which is very repulsive and wants every penny you earn as an Atacadão replenisher. Warhammer 3 offers you some factions and some lords, but to play with the others you need to have the other 2 previous games and some more DLCs to unlock others, like the Beastmen. But don't be fooled when you buy the games and DLCs, you don't get all the lords. The game has a million DLCs just with lords. Does each gentleman change the gameplay? Yes. But you still need to pay for a good part of them. This ends up alienating most players, especially Brazilians. But, to be quite honest, just by having the base game you already have a lot of factions and lords to test. If you want to play with more factions, I recommend that you just pick up the other 2 games. With them you will already have about 70 of what the game can offer. I give the game a 7.8 rating. It's wonderful and, if you're not careful, you'll drop your course, college and quit your job just to live in the world of this game. The shifts go by and you don't even notice. However, this monetization system is very repulsive in my opinion. If it weren't for that, I would give it an 8.8. I hope it becomes more famous here in Brazil and that its Brazilian community grows a lot.

review:
the game is complex with out being strategic.   I't not about using the mechanics in the game for an outcome its more just hanging on longer than the Ai which turns a corner at some point and you start to win.  the worst thing you can do is actually try to win the game. 
If the answer is mostly:

survive the opening script
exploit AI weaknesses
wait for the snowball
The strategic depth is shallower than it appears.

the propsed strategice mechanics feel less like a strategic challenge and more like a scoreboard tracking things that happen around you.
For example:

A diplomacy system where nobody ever accepts deals.
A trade system that contributes almost nothing.
A loyalty system that never actually affects behavior.
The mechanic might as well not exist.

review:
Покупая данную игру, Вы автоматически попадаете в мышеловку. 
Прежде, чем прощупать весь контент столь чудесной игры, вам нужно приобрести прошлые части, весь жанр превосходен - спору нет, но с одним нюансом. 
Я лично фанат стратегий и поиграл немало в другие стратегии, однако Вархаммер: Купи Все ДЛС 3 показала мне, что и они могут быть интересными, динамичными и затягивающими, чего стоит один дизайн всей масштабной карты, идеальная проработка локаций (не похожи друг на друга), модели проработаны до деталей, а бои - масштабные и эпичные, насыщенность и контраст вливается в жанр Вахи. 

Уникальная вариативность геймплея, большое количество фракций (у которых свой индивидуальный подход для игроков). Каждая раса проработана до мелких частиц, начиная от визуала - заканчивая озвучкой, благодаря всему этому - попросту можно погрузиться в атмосферу игры. 

К сожалению, я попался в данную мышеловку и теперь, чтобы прощупать весь контент игры, мне необходимо приобрести ДЛС. (не буду) 

Помимо цен, хочу обратить внимание на оптимизацию, она около нулевая, игре если не ошибаюсь более 4х лет, оптимизации все еще нет.

review: i didnt like the tutorial tbh, but immortal empires has been much better so far. I like how each faction has their own gameplay mechanic. That said, UI is clunky, the camera is garbage (seriously.. even mods don't fix it) and some parts of the game just feel like they're being held together like a decade old spaghetti monolith of code, kind of like a bethesda game. Game is also visually a mixed bag, LoD and draw distance is terrible, some battlefields look amazing and others look like a ps2 game. Also not a fan of having to use mods to fix annoyances because the mods become outdated and dont always work well together. Other than that so far its been good and there's a lot to like. Hope they make a wh4 at some point though, i'm not sure 40k lends itself well for this type of game.

Оценивая игру после первых двух частей могу сказать только одно - это охуенно. По сравнению со второй частью был допилен каждый аспект: больше масштаба на карте, больше фракций, лордов, юнитов, механик, событий, лучше графика, даже оптимизация лучше чем во второй части - говорю как обладатель бюджетного ноутбука, тройка реально идет лучше двойки при тех же настройках графики, видимо все-таки игроки наныли и они реально оптимизировали игру.

review:
토탈워 시리즈는 처음 입문이 어렵다 느껴진다.
허나 점점 늘어나는 종족과 편의성 패치들과 뒤늦게라도 정신차린 CA의 모습이 이 게임을 믿고 구매해도 될 정도로 만들었다.

이 게임엔 칼, 활, 총, 마법, 탱크, 기마병, 박격포, 거대괴수, 용 등등 판타지 뽕맛을 채워주는 수 많은 요소가 있고 이는 종족들마다 각 특성이 다르기에 오랜시간 붙들고 질리지 않게 게임을 즐기기 최적화 되어있다.

review:
I've played a few hundred hours of Total Warhammer across the three iterations, and I want to start by saying that there is entertainment value to be had here...if you're willing to pay the enormous cost of entry and do the work necessary to make up for the shortcomings of the game's lackluster, decade-old engine. At this point, SEGA and CA have long since abandoned spending development time on making meaningful improvements to the game. They're just churning out new Legendary Lords at $7.99 each. They've been doing this for quite some time, and by now the game feels pretty bloated and ramshackle.

If you want to be able to play with all of the various factions and lords that are available after ten years, you're going to need to shell out hundreds of dollars. Even on sale. After doing that, you'll have purchased a game that is really starting to show its age. It's not well optimized. The AI is a complete joke to play against. There are still some frustrating bugs lurking around, and it's very unlikely that any of this will be fixed any time soon. 

The game looks incredible on decent hardware, and the battles are a joy to engage with for any fan of the Warhammer universe or the Total War franchise. The tactical level is where this game really shines, but the strategic/campaign layer is almost painful to work through at this stage. There are mods on the Workshop that you can get to mitigate some of the problems with this game, but honestly, trying to cobble together the right combination of them feels like trying to plug holes in a sinking ship. 

Total Warhammer 40K is right around the corner, with a brand new engine that looks absolutely fantastic. If I were you, I'd save your money and buy that when it launches. I'm sure that they will monetize the hell out of it, just like they do with this, but at least it'll be built from the ground up to work on modern hardware and incorporate everything they learned making this.

review_english: New to the game? I advise you to play as Emperor Karl Franz! The game so, unfortunately, all your allies from the Empire faction were destroyed five moves after the start of the game, and now all the nearby armies are heading in your direction to wipe you off the face of the earth. Oh, and did I mention that your troops are some of the weakest in the game at this stage? gordik neural network am I crazy or is this madness neural network The Empire is in the center of the map and is attacked from all sides. Nearby are vampires, Nurgle, beastmen, orcs and other aggressive factions. The allied electors are constantly losing and require salvation. The death of the Electors reduces the Authority of the Empire. Prestige constantly has to be spent on extinguishing conflicts. You cannot seize allied lands without serious penalties. The economy does not allow for a long time to maintain several strong armies. The basic infantry of the Empire is weaker than many of its neighbors. The best troops are unlocked only at later stages of development. Karl Franz is recommended for beginners due to his clear and classic army, but in practice his campaign in Immortal Empires is one of the most stressful and difficult in the entire game.

review: Игра неплохая, минусы лично для меня как относительно нового игрока в W3 это : перегруженный интерфейс, куча всякой нудятины которую надо закрывать и прятать, цветовая палитра и сам визуал ( поймите правильно) Юниты и модельки персонажей выглядят на хорошо гд-то 7 из 10, но окружение просто и задний фон просто ужасен, даже в 2к на ультра настройках видно лесенки, и очень малополигонов у всего, трава, деревья, камни, небо и т.д... что касательно геймплея сами файты очень медленные, знаю это фишка тоталов играл и в рим и в британию с сёгуном... но там это как-то уместно и интересно, а в вахе где есть магия куча различных юнитов хочется видеть больше экшена( мясца как говорится -_- но по факту всё как в масле ( Главные герои в слоумо в гуще сражения неторопливо махают своими кувалдами и плавно раскидывают окружение) магия, скилы кастуются аналогично медленно и нудно... плюсов у игры огромное количество, а я решил написать именно о минусах данной игры. Нет критики нет развития.

review_english: The game is not bad, the downsides for me personally as a relatively new player in W3 are the overloaded interface, a bunch of all sorts of tedious things that need to be closed and hidden, the color palette and the visuals themselves (understand correctly) Units and character models look good about 7 out of 10, but the environment is simple and the background is simply terrible, even in 2k on ultra settings you can see stairs, and there are very few polygons for everything, grass, trees, stones, sky, etc... as for the gameplay, the fights themselves are very slow, I know this is a feature of totals, I played both in Rome and in Britain with the shogun... but there it is somehow appropriate and interesting, and in the battle where there is magic there are a lot of different units, I want to see more action (meat as they say -_- but in fact everything is like butter (The main characters in slowmo in the thick of the battle leisurely wave their with sledgehammers and smoothly scatter the surroundings) magic, skills are cast similarly slowly and tediously... the game has a huge number of advantages, but I decided to write specifically about the disadvantages of this game. There is no criticism, no development.

review:
The game is very enjoyable, and one of the best experiences for warhammer fantasy games I ever had. But Creative Assembly's horrible DLC policy makes this probably the worst total war game to get into by far. Unless you are prepared to spend over 300 pounds, you'll have maybe a quarter of factions to choose from. 

The game runs surprisingly fine for me (on a macbook pro), but has some serious texture overlay issues. I have not experienced any other major technical issues aside from very few crashes. 
The game has the Kingdoms of Ind and the Hinterlands of Khuresh present on the map but even with the addition of Bhashiva I highly doubt these two regions would be receiving DLC. Who knows? Maybe they'll add them in an overpriced racepack in five years time. 

If you adore warhammer fantasy, you'll enjoy the game. If you would like to get into warhammer fantasy, don't do it through this game. Though it does prepare you for the ridiculous pricing of the tabletop. And with the upcoming release of total war wh40k, I trust that the combined prices of dlc in that game will overshadow whatever CA did with this game.

review:
这是全战系列的垃圾作品，而且毫无疑问。
1.全战罗马2，阿提拉里弓骑兵的侧向和后向射击能力被取消了，导致战锤3的骑射只能移动射击正面，这就导致如果你的弓骑兵选择避免近战的选项，遇到对手骑射一旦后撤就只能被动挨打。这个改动等于直接废掉了所有的骑射兵种。
2.虽然有仆从的外交选项，但你无法大量使用仆从兵种，而战锤3各派系没有一个派系兵种齐全，不是少空中战力就是少重火力，想玩各种打法只能开新档，貌似游戏厂商黔驴技穷了，只能用这种方法来突显各派系特点。
3.相对罗马2，阿提拉，没有群组阵型选项，抗线的步兵也没有单独的盾阵枪阵之类的技能，居然时不时会有被对方强行透过，反常识。
所以战锤123，除了构架于魔法怪物的非现实世界有点小期待，玩法不但没进步，相较十年前产品罗马2，阿提拉还倒退了。
4.然后是游戏策划努力想拿来做卖点的画面，画质拿出来我只能说不忍直视~~~没眼看啊，想买的最好有心理准备。
5.这家公司吃相太难看，一个游戏他分开来卖，换个地图变出123三代作品，一个派系当一个DLC，而且每一个都不便宜。良心实在是坏！！！
........................................................
还有一个全战系列一直都有的BUG，比如我一个骑兵单位攻击对方，最前面的几个骑兵小人接触对手后，单位里的其他小人就不动了，站着看戏，我只能选择让这个骑兵单位继续前进透过对方单位的办法让闲置的兵模小人和对手接触从而触发战斗。这个BUG一直都在，全系列都有，厂商没发现还是不屑于修改?

review_english: This is a garbage work in the Total War series, and there is no doubt that 1. Total War Rome 2 Attilari Archer's side and rear shooting capabilities were canceled, resulting in Warhammer 3's mounted archer being able to move and shoot frontally. This means that if your archer chooses the option to avoid melee combat, it will only be passively beaten when it encounters an opponent's mounted archer and retreats. This change is equivalent to directly abolishing all mounted archer units. 2. Although there is a diplomatic option for servants, you cannot use servant units in large numbers. However, none of the factions in Warhammer 3 has complete troops. It either lacks air combat capabilities or lacks heavy firepower. If you want to play various playing styles, you can only open a new gear. It seems that the game manufacturer has run out of skills and can only use this method to highlight the characteristics of each faction. 3. Compared with Rome 2 Attila, there is no group formation option, and the infantry resisting the line does not have skills such as individual shield formations and gun formations. From time to time, they are forced through by the opponent, which is contrary to common sense. Therefore, Warhammer 123 Except for the unrealistic world built around magical monsters, I have little expectations. Not only has the gameplay not improved, but it has also regressed compared to the product of Rome 2 Attila ten years ago. 4. Then there are the pictures that the game planners tried hard to use as a selling point. When the picture quality is taken out, I can only say that it is unbearable to look at it. It is unsightly. It is best to be mentally prepared if you want to buy it. 5. This company is too ugly. It sells a game separately and changes the map to create a 123 third-generation work. One faction is a DLC, and each one is not cheap. I have a really bad conscience. .................................................. There is also a BUG that has always been present in the Total War series. For example, one of my cavalry units attacks the opponent. After the first few cavalry minion contact the opponent, the other minion in the unit will stop moving. Stand and watch the show. I can only choose to let this cavalry unit continue to move forward through the opponent's unit to let the idle soldier minion come into contact with the opponent to trigger the battle. This BUG has always been there in the entire series. The manufacturer has not discovered it or bothered to modify it?

review:
A chier !
Aucun équilibrage toujours plus de power creep, pour vendre ce "jeu" (enfin si on considère qu'un jeu n'ai pas forcément divertissant) ainsi que leurs DLC bien évidement, qui repousse a chaque nouvelle sortie les limites de la connerie.... enfin je veux dire du power creep.
Vous serez obligé de les acheté pour pouvoir joué sans vomir du sang a chaque fin de tour, et encore plus si vous voulez réussir une campagne.
Des bugs qui ne seront probablement jamais corriger, une "IA" toujours plus catastrophique: triche, bugs,  focus du joueur par les faction IA (pour rappel CA avais résolu ce problème sur warhammer 2 avant de le remettre 2 ou 3 semaine plus tard....), ect....

Reste a espéré qu'une fois tous les DLC sortie CA ce sorte les doigts du cul pour corrigé et amélioré ce jeu qui a un gros potentiel.

Quand a wahammer 40K, il a lair très tentent.... cependant ! il y a un très gros risque qu'ils applique 
la même politique de power creep pour vendre les DLC, sans oublié que sur 40K le potentiel de DLC est bien plus important que sur les warhammer battle donc....

Avec 200 h sur le 1, 1300 h sur le 2 et 1100 sur le 3.... j'ai de moins en moins envie d'y joué alors que j'ai adoré les 2 premiers (au point d'acheté tout les DLC, qui était très bon a l'époque), mais aujourd'hui chaque présentation de DLC s'accompagne d'un soupire suivi d'un "mouais, bah c'est encore plus con et absurde que le précédent."
Je sais pas vous mais pour m'a part, pas question de lâché le moindre centime dans les prochain DLC et plus tard dans total war warhammer 40K.

review:
Я никогда не была фанатом стратегий, однако Total War: Warhammer III показала мне, что и они могут быть интересными, динамичными и затягивающими.

Больше всего меня порадовало количество фракций, а следовательно, вариативность геймплея. Каждая раса также идеально проработана визуально и аудиально, благодаря чему проникнуться её атмосферой очень просто. К сожалению, чтобы насладиться этим аспектом, придётся прибегнуть к 'покупке' DLC. Цены, к слову, очень кусаются, что разочаровывает.

Игра безумно красивая. Локации не похожи друг на друга, и все очень хороши, модельки проработаны до деталей, а бои - масштабные и эпичные.

Из минусов, помимо цен на DLC, стоит отметить оптимизацию. Удивительно, что за столько лет игра ещё не отполирована.

Игра определённо заслуживает того, чтобы дать ей шанс. Она очень затягивает.

review_english: I've never been a fan of strategy games, but Total War Warhammer III showed me that they can be interesting, dynamic and addictive. What pleased me most was the number of factions, and therefore the variability of gameplay. Each race is also perfectly designed visually and auditorily, making it very easy to get into its atmosphere. Unfortunately, to enjoy this aspect you will have to resort to 'purchasing' the DLC. Prices, by the way, are very high, which is disappointing. The game is incredibly beautiful. The locations are not similar to each other, and they are all very good, the models are worked out in detail, and the battles are large-scale and epic. Among the minuses, in addition to the prices for DLC, it is worth noting the optimization. It's surprising that after so many years the game has not yet been polished. The game is definitely worth giving it a chance. It's very addictive.

review:
Worst new player experience I've ever had.

You get acces to like 5 factions, with their rosters heavily limited some of which to the point of being basically unusable (like the tzench) since they are missing core units. The game is also programmed to just piss you off with "anti-player bias" and it feels like if you don't look up and follow an exact guide on how to play the first 10-20 turns you will just get obliterated. Make the mistake of playing on easy? The game is going to straight up lie to you about battles and make  you feel like a complete worthless idiot when you can't win a battle it claims is a decisive victory with low losses. I spent nearly 100 hours trying to learn brettonians (Simply because they are the only faction that doesnt have half their roster taken away from them by default) and despite 10+ additional hours looking at guides and trying to follow them exactly. I just can't reproduce anything. The AI does not behave like it does in those videos. This game is probably really fun, and probably quit easy, to people who have been playing these games for over a decade but trying to play anything other than grand cathay as a fresh player with no DLCs or previous titles was an absolute nightmare and worst gaming experiences I've ever had.

This game isn't even worth the $8 I paid for it, let alone the over $300+ they want just to unlock everything in the game.

review:
Game's great and is my favorite Total War as someone who has played all of the old ones. However, its greatness is the portrayal of a massive world with many unique characters and good lore. The actual gameplay itself is a bit unbalanced and not very nuanced at times - which is a consequence of the fantasy setting mixing with Total War gameplay.

Magic or certain monsters can make thousands of infantry completely useless when you're fighting them, or certain things can make your armies useless which can be infuriating. It's also extremely easy to cheese/exploit certain AI behaviors and you'll do them inadvertently.

So, for people looking for a "Total War" from a strategical standpoint, I would say something like Shogun 2 with mods might be a lot better. For people who are fans of fantasy (I like both aspects, but I'm quite the fantasy massif) - this game is an ultimate fantasy game. You've got essentially a whole planet full of lore and all the races you can think of. A lot to immerse in, on a beautifully made campaign map, which is why I do find this game a masterpiece from that point of view.

There are also no modern "newspeak" (so to say) insanities or particularly ugly characters. Our modern society is on a downfall, and we can see that the new 40k TW title will be heavily affected. This just barely escaped it so I would say that it is likely the last good Total War game as well. Amazing mod support, too (albeit there are too many parentless degenerates making them as well, beware of Nexusmods in particular). 

Absolutely recommended for people looking to immerse themselves in a fantasy world.

review:
I have a list of complaints:
-All your favorite table-top units are missing their normal spectacle and character thanks to CA not taking the time to include unit command groups (champions, standard bearers, etc.).
-The vanilla climate and corruption designs are entirely defanged to the point where it may as well not even exist, in favor of giving people and easy game.
-A lack of unit type limits means you will routinely see absurdly stupid stuff like an army of 15 catapults and 1 hero.

But mods make the game playable and I'll give them a thumbs up for CA getting half-way to the goal.

review: Poorly designed AI constantly cheat (as in they do things that are literally impossible for you to do when you control the same army). Yes there are ways round it. but if you have to basically use exploits and cheese with a lot of the factions instead of actual tactics just to play the game (and research all the cheesy meta instead of actually being a better strategist), that means the developer has dropped the ball. I still like playing the game to an extent, I just hate the "one rule for me and another for thee" band aid that gets slapped over the AI to cover the fact that they are poorly designed. I don't like being forced to use meta cheese instead of actual strategy, it makes the game substantially less fun.

review:
DO NOT BUY. I am a long time fan of Total War going way back, some of my favorite games. That has been completely ruined by this company. This is the second time in a row where they punish you for not buying the DLC. When I mean punish I mean they give the AI all the top-tier units but you do not have access to the necessary units to counter them. If you like lizardmen, you need to buy 3 DLCs to have their full roster! 

You buy the game just to see you have 3 factions unlocked, the rest are all greyed out, you go to one of the few factions you have access to and see that half the lords are greyed out, and then you start playing one you do have access to, only to learn a few hours in that you don't have access to the top half of the unit tree. 

This is wrong and they know it. Do not buy this game unless its 10 years from now and you can get the full bundle for $10.

Diplomacy between other races, Mass battles where you can lead the troops as a real commander... there are also many races from Humans, monsters, elves, demons... and during the game, by concluding a military or protective alliance with other factions, you will be able to build an outpost on their territory and you will have the opportunity to order their soldiers. review:
After almost a thousand hours playing Warhammer3, (this is not including the time in 1 and 2), I thought I would throw a positive review in. 
You have to like playing Total War games. If you haven't played RTS games like this before, try it, but be prepared to study the game like learning chess. This is what I like about it!
Hundreds of unique units, and characters, and damage types affected by scenario and environment... and that's just the bit of the complexity. A lot to understand to have a positive experience. Amazing.
Definitely room to improve with the AI, both the campaign and battle decision making. There some things that don't work as described, and some things that have been bugged since launch. Not perfect, but if you like Warhammer lore, like real-time battles, and have the patience of a saint to wait on the 250 factions to take their turns... this game is awesome!

review:
Chiant à mourir. Une déception totale.
Reçu gratuitement. j'aurais préféré ne jamais l'avoir dans ma bibliothèque. Ce jeu est horrible et devient de pire en pire.
Le gameplay se résume à des factions ennemies ultra pétées qui spawn en permanence de nulle part au milieu de ton territoire pour tout détruire avant de disparaître. Zéro stratégie, juste de la frustration pure.
Ajoutez à ça une optimisation absolument désastreuse et vous obtenez le pire Total War de la franchise. Les avis positifs sont incompréhensibles.
Ne perdez ni votre temps ni votre argent, restez sur les anciens opus. Je recommande pas du tout.

Zéro stratégie, juste de la frustration pure. Ajoutez à ça une optimisation absolument désastreuse et vous obtenez le pire Total War de la franchise. Les avis positifs sont incompréhensibles. Ne perdez ni votre temps ni votre argent, restez sur les anciens opus. Je recommande pas du tout.
review_english: Boring as hell. A total disappointment. Received for free. I would have preferred never to have it in my library. This game is horrible and gets worse and worse. The gameplay boils down to super crazy enemy factions that constantly spawn out of nowhere in the middle of your territory to destroy everything before disappearing. Zero strategy, just pure frustration. Add to that absolutely disastrous optimization and you get the worst Total War in the franchise. The positive reviews are incomprehensible. Don't waste your time or money, stick to the old releases. I don't recommend it at all.

review:
A fantastic strategy game buried under an absurd amount of DLC. The base experience is enjoyable, but if you want what feels like the "complete" version, be prepared to spend hundreds of euros/dollars on additional content.

The scale, faction variety, and battles are excellent, but the monetization has become increasingly difficult to justify. Asking players to invest this much money into a single title is hard to defend, no matter how good the core game is.

If this is the business model planned for TW: Warhammer 40K, count me out.

review:
If you want to play with all the units of any race, you have to buy the base game (which includes races you might not want to play as), an army DLC (unless it's part of the base game), and several lord packs (that also include content for races you might not want to play as, and would have to buy the base army for anyway). You have to do that to access units and lords that exist in the base game, and the AI factions will use, but are put behind a paywall for the player - just to tempt you into buying them. You then have in-game advertising for the DLC...and annoying little flame icons promoting the latest carved out chunk of the game in the start menus. 
 I'm not sure how much worse it could get without them adding loot-box gambling, or cash-for-cosmetics, to the mix.

It's annoying because the actual game is quite good. I just don't want to give them any more money for that sort of foulness.

review:
It is a great TOTAL WAR. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Se amate la strategia profonda, la gestione millimetrica e le battaglie campali epiche, questo titolo è un acquisto obbligato. Attualmente è l'apice della serie: credo sinceramente che solo un futuro Medieval Total War 3 potrà fare di meglio.

⏳ Longevità: 95/100 – Monumentale. La presenza di così tante fazioni uniche e l'accesso a ben due campagne completamente differenti (con obiettivi e mappe diverse) garantisce una rigiocabilità praticamente infinita. Superare il turno 100 è solo l'inizio.

🎮 Gameplay: 90/100 – Solido, appagante e strategico. Le battaglie richiedono vera tattica e la gestione dell'economia sulla mappa è profonda. La formula Total War qui esprime il suo massimo potenziale.

🎨 Grafica: 90/100 – Tutto funziona a dovere. Il livello di dettaglio dei modelli, gli effetti sul campo e la pulizia visiva generale regalano un impatto visivo eccellente in ogni scontro.

🧠 Curva di Apprendimento (Learning Curve): 60/100 – Non è un gioco immediato. Per iniziare a padroneggiare davvero le meccaniche, l'economia delle province e i punti di forza di ogni fazione, mettete in conto di doverci giocare almeno 50 ore. Ma ogni ora investita viene ampiamente ripagata.

🛡️ Stabilità: Bugs Free – L'esperienza di gioco è solida e pulita. Il codice è stabile, permettendoti di goderti lunghe sessioni di gioco senza la frustrazione di crash improvvisi o bug che rompono la campagna.

Voto Complessivo: Assolutamente consigliato.

review:
Birçok kişi ek paket almak gerektiğini 1. ve 2. oyuna sahip olmak gerektiğini söylemiş zaten. Bu konuda size başka bi tüyo vermek istiyorum. Bazı sitelerden steam koduyla oyunu uygun fiyata satın alabiliyorsunuz. Ama asıl olay şu ki; bi tane site var bu konuda tekelleşmiş, kodların satıldığı siteler için ekstra %10-20 arası indirim kodları oluyor. Liste şeklinde bakıp en uyguna hangisi oluyor görüp fiyatları karşılaştırabiliyorsunuz. Ve yanındaki linke tıklayıp kod satılan bilindik sitelere kolayca ulaşıyorsunuz. Oyunumuza dönersek eskiden bu 'Şaheser'e verecek 10binlerce liram yok diye maalesef korsan oynardım. Kodla uyguna alabileceğimi fark edince 3oyunu birlikte aldım ve 1.5k tl falandı, şaka değil. :) Sadece ana oyun değil, ek paket (faction-lider) kodları falan da var. Ek paketlerin bazılarını %50 indirim olunca direkt steam'den aldım. Sonuç olarak 2.5k tlye falan oyunun çoğuna, factionlara ve ırklara sahip olmuş oldum. Yalnız uyarmam gerek satın alırken doğru region kodunu almak önemli. Farklı oyun platform kodları almamaya da dikkat edin. Türkiye için Ortadoğu regionu için uygundu diye hatırlıyorum, alırken siz gene de kontrol edin. Gider Avrupa kodu alırsanız paranız çöp olur. Aynı şeyi Crimson Desert'ta da yaşadım. 40dolara falan almıştım. Meseleye de orada uyandım zaten. Ülkemizin içine düştüğü ekonomik durumlar bizi maalesef böyle şeylere itiyor. Ortadoğulu olduk iyice. 
Sonuca gelirsek bu oyun harika, iyi bir sistem ve iyi bir monitörle haritada ordunuzu sağa sola taşırken bile renk paletine hayran kalıp, sisli atmosferin içinde kayboluyorsunuz. Oynarken ben şahsen unutuyorum Ortadoğulu olduğumu. Tavsiye edilir.

review_english: Many people have already said that it is necessary to buy the additional package and have the 1st and 2nd games. I want to give you another tip on this subject. You can buy the game at an affordable price with a steam code from some sites. But the main thing is that there is one site that has a monopoly on this issue. There are 10-20 extra discount codes for the sites where the codes are sold. You can look at it as a list and see which one is the most affordable and compare the prices. And by clicking on the link next to it, you can easily access well-known sites that sell codes. Going back to our game, unfortunately, I used to play pirates because I didn't have 10 thousand lira to spend on this 'Masterpiece'. When I realized that I could buy it cheaply with a code, I bought all 3 games together and it was around 1.5k TL, no joke. ) Not only the main game, but also additional pack (faction-leader) codes etc. I bought some of the additional packages directly from Steam when there was a 50% discount. As a result, I owned most of the game, factions and races for around 2.5k TL. I just have to warn you that it is important to get the correct region code when purchasing. Also be careful not to buy different game platform codes. I remember that it was suitable for Türkiye and the Middle East region, so check it when you buy it. If you go and buy a European code, your money will be wasted. I experienced the same thing in Crimson Desert. I bought it for like 40 dollars. That's where I woke up to the issue. Unfortunately, the economic situations that our country is in push us towards such things. We have become Middle Eastern. As for the conclusion, this game is great, with a good system and a good monitor, you will be amazed by the color palette and get lost in the foggy atmosphere, even as you move your army left and right on the map. While playing, I personally forget that I am from the Middle East. It is recommended.

review:
Le plus complet de la trilogie. La campagne est plus ambitieuse, avec une carte énorme et des factions très variées qui changent vraiment la manière de jouer.

Les batailles sont spectaculaires et très dynamiques, avec encore plus de monstres, de magie et d’unités uniques que dans les précédents.

Par contre, l’équilibrage et l’intelligence de l’IA restent irréguliers, et la campagne peut parfois devenir répétitive sur la durée.

C’est le meilleur point d’entrée de la trilogie aujourd’hui, surtout si tu veux du contenu massif et de la variété.

Les batailles sont spectaculaires et très dynamiques, avec encore plus de monstres, de magie et d unités uniques que dans les précédents. Par contre, l équilibrage et l intelligence de l IA restent irréguliers, et la campagne peut parfois devenir répétitive sur la durée. C est le meilleur point d entrée de la trilogie aujourd hui, surtout si tu veux du contenu massif et de la variété.
review_english: The most complete of the trilogy. The campaign is more ambitious, with a huge map and very varied factions that really change the way you play. The battles are spectacular and very dynamic, with even more monsters, magic and unique units than in the previous ones. On the other hand, the balancing and intelligence of the AI ​​remain irregular, and the campaign can sometimes become repetitive over time. This is the best entry point to the trilogy today, especially if you want massive content and variety.

review:
臣本边关一武夫，世守长垣，披甲执戈，与妖邪血战于朔漠之间。蒙天朝不弃，受龙帝圣恩，列于将帅之班，赐兵符印绶，统三军之众。恩深似海，重若昆仑，臣虽驽钝，未敢一日忘怀。

昔龙帝开天辟地，定鼎九州，筑万里长垣以御混沌，庇护万民。万载以来，妖氛屡起，邪神窥伺，然天朝将士前赴后继，血洒边陲，故神州得以长存。今北地风雪蔽天，魔军压境，长垣震动，亿万黎民皆系于此一战。

臣闻，食君之禄，当报君之恩；受国之庇，当守国之门。龙帝以社稷托臣，臣岂敢惜此残躯？大丈夫立于天地之间，当知忠义二字重于性命。臣纵无经天纬地之才，亦知忠臣不事二主，烈士不更其节。

若妖魔至城下，则执戈而战；若城垣将倾，则以血补之；若三军尽没，则臣当独立城头，以身殉国。玉可碎而白不可改，竹可焚而节不可毁。身虽死于乱军之中，忠魂亦当化作长垣之石，永镇北疆。

臣尝闻：天子守国门，君王死社稷。此非一人之誓，乃震旦万载之魂也。国若存，则万民安；国若亡，则九州俱灭。故臣宁为长垣下之枯骨，不为混沌前之降臣。

今妖星临空，邪神窥世，外夷压境于长垣之外。臣请率三军将士，同赴国难。凡执剑者执剑，执枪者执枪，凡震旦子民，皆当共守河山。臣愿殚精竭虑，护少帝之统，卫龙庭之威；纵马革裹尸，粉身碎骨，亦无悔于今日之誓。

若臣退半步，愿天地共弃之；
若臣降一言，愿祖宗共诛之；
若臣负天朝，愿魂魄永堕混沌之渊。

此身可灭，此志不可夺；
长垣可毁，天朝不可降！

臣谨以血书此誓，愿与神州共存亡。

review_english: I am a warrior at the border gate. I have been guarding the long wall for a long time. I wore armor and held a gun. I fought bloody battles with evil spirits in Shuo Mo. I was not abandoned by the Heavenly Dynasty. I was favored by the Dragon Emperor and was listed in the general class. I was given a talisman and a ribbon to unify the three armies. My kindness is as deep as the sea and as heavy as Kunlun. Although I am dull, I dare not forget it for a day. In the past, the Dragon Emperor created the world and established the nine states. He built thousands of miles of long walls to ward off chaos and protect all people. Over the past ten thousand years, the evil atmosphere has arisen repeatedly, and the evil gods have been watching. However, the soldiers of the Chinese dynasty have come forward one after another, and their blood has been shed on the borders. Therefore, China can survive forever. Today, the northern land is covered with wind and snow. You should know that the word loyalty is more important than life. Even if you have no knowledge of the world, you also know that a loyal minister will not serve the two masters. Martyrs will not change their integrity. If the demon comes to the city, fight with arms. If the city wall is about to collapse, use blood to repair it. If the three armies are wiped out, then I will serve as an independent city head and sacrifice myself for the country. Jade can be broken but white cannot be changed. Bamboo can be burned but its knot cannot be destroyed. Even if the body dies in the rebel army, the loyal soul should be turned into the stone of the long wall. Forever guarding the northern border, I have heard that the emperor guards the gate of the country, and the king dies in the country. This is not an oath made by one person, but the soul of Cathay for thousands of years. If the country survives, all the people will be safe. If the country perishes, all the nine states will be destroyed. Therefore, I would rather be a withered bone under the long wall than a surrender before the chaos. Now the demon star is in the sky, and the evil god is peeking into the world. Foreign barbarians are pressing outside the long wall. I ask you to lead the three armies and soldiers to fight against the national disaster. Those who hold swords hold swords, and those who hold spears hold spears. All the people of Cathay should guard the rivers and mountains together. I am willing to work hard to protect the throne of the young emperor and defend the power of Longting. Even if my body is wrapped in horse leather and my body is shattered into pieces, I have no regrets about today's oath. If I step back half a step, I hope that heaven and earth will abandon me. If I make a word, I hope my ancestors will kill me together. If I betray the heaven, I hope my soul will fall into the abyss of chaos forever. This body can be destroyed, this will cannot be taken away. The long wall can be destroyed, but the heaven cannot surrender. I would like to write this oath in blood, willing to live and die with China.

review:
The game has monetization issues, with some factions only being playable with the DLC units. But I don't mind this, the base game (all 3 combined, for the immortal empires campaign) has more than enough to offer.

So many different factions to play with, and you don't have to buy the DLC if you don't like it.

Gameplay is amazing, both the overview map & the combat itself. The AI is decent, and the variety is really good.

The only complaint I have with this game (and most others of it's type) is how the endgame is boring and the goal isn't obtainable without a huge time sink / cheese.
